keyword.searcher_intent: Understand what the ONA exam is, how it differs from other exams, what to include in portfolio, and how to prepare for the interview

audience.pain_summary: Confused about ONA because it's portfolio-based, not a test. Don't know what to include. Worried about the final interview.

angle: ONA is the most misunderstood part of inburgering because it's not an exam at all — it's a portfolio plus interview. This guide demystifies the entire process.
thesis_sentence: The ONA (Orientation on the Dutch Labour Market) isn't a test you study for — it's a portfolio you build over time plus a 30-minute interview, and understanding this difference is key to not being caught off guard.

outline.section: ONA: The 'Exam' That Isn't an Exam
outline.notes: Hook with the key difference. ONA has no multiple choice, no timed writing, no recorded speaking. It's portfolio-based with a final interview. Most people don't realize this until too late.
ONA = Oriëntatie op de Nederlandse Arbeidsmarkt (Orientation on the Dutch Labour Market). The 6th component of inburgering. Required for people under the Wi 2013 legislation.
Not everyone. Required if you started under Wi 2013 (before 2022). Check your DUO account or PIP to confirm. Wi 2021 participants have different requirements.

outline.section: The Two Paths: 64-Hour Course or Portfolio + Interview
outline.notes: Option 1: Complete 64 hours of ONA course at approved provider. Option 2: Build portfolio and pass final interview. Most self-study people choose the interview path.

outline.section: What Goes in Your ONA Portfolio
outline.notes: Required documents: Dutch-format CV, evidence of job search activities (applications, emails, screenshots), networking/event attendance proof, personal action plan, any other engagement evidence.

outline.section: The Final Interview: What to Expect
outline.notes: 30-minute interview with 2 DUO representatives. They ask about your portfolio documents. 6-week waiting time after submitting. Conducted in Dutch at your level.

outline.section: Why You Can't Cram for ONA
outline.notes: Key insight: ONA requires activities spread over weeks/months. Job applications, networking events, CV development — these take time. Start early or you'll rush at the end.

outline.section: ONA Exemptions: If You Already Work
outline.notes: Good news: if you've worked 48+ hours/month for 6+ months in the past year as an employee in NL, you can apply for exemption. Freelancers/ZZP may not qualify.
ONA costs €50 to take. Lower than language exams. If you fail the interview, you can retake.
Starting too late, weak portfolio documentation, not practicing interview questions in Dutch, forgetting networking evidence. Practical tips to avoid each.
Recommended: start building portfolio 6 months before you want to complete ONA. Earlier is better. Activities need to feel organic, not rushed.

value_props_to_weave.pain: ONA interview is conducted in Dutch and they're not confident
value_props_to_weave.prop: Speaking practice builds confidence for any Dutch conversation
value_props_to_weave.proof: Interview is conversational, not formal — similar to daily speaking practice

value_props_to_weave.pain: Overwhelmed by all the different inburgering components
value_props_to_weave.prop: Understanding each component clearly reduces anxiety
value_props_to_weave.proof: ONA is actually the most flexible component — no time pressure on the 'exam'

originality_nuggets: Most ONA content is buried in official documents. This is a clear walkthrough.
Specific portfolio contents and interview format details

seo.title_tag: ONA Exam Guide: Portfolio, Interview & How to Pass (2026)
seo.meta_description: The ONA exam isn't a test — it's a portfolio plus interview. Complete guide to what goes in your portfolio, what to expect in the interview, and exemptions.

aeo_directives.featured_snippet_target: What is the ONA exam for inburgering?
aeo_directives.definition_paragraph: The ONA (Oriëntatie op de Nederlandse Arbeidsmarkt) is the sixth component of the Dutch inburgering exam, focusing on orientation to the Dutch labour market. Unlike the language and KNM exams, ONA is not a traditional test. Instead, you either complete 64 hours of approved ONA courses, or build a portfolio documenting your job market activities and pass a 30-minute final interview. The portfolio should include a Dutch-format CV, evidence of job search activities, networking attendance, and a personal action plan. If you've worked 48+ hours per month for 6+ months in the Netherlands, you may qualify for exemption.
faq_questions: What is the ONA exam for inburgering?
What goes in an ONA portfolio?
How do I pass the ONA interview?
Can I get an ONA exemption if I work?
How long does ONA take to complete?
